assumption_text: The civilian Golan denominator is restricted to residents physically present on the occupied plateau battle area rather than all northern-Israel civilians affected by shelling.
rationale: A stable, auditable polygon-wide resident count was located only for the plateau population, not for the wider northern-Israel shelling footprint.

assumption_text: Average Israeli troop exposure during 6-9 October is modeled above the opening 7,000 because reserve units were arriving during the episode.
rationale: An opening-force count exists, but not a day-by-day troop table. The average-force approach better matches the rapidly changing mobilization picture.

assumption_text: A majority of Golan-front military deaths occurred in the 6-9 October window.
rationale: Kichen treats the opening as a four-day decisive fight and marks 9 October as the turning point; the later counteroffensive is therefore separated instead of blended.

assumption_text: Average Israeli troops in the opening Sinai window exceed the opening garrison of 18,000 because reserve reinforcements and additional divisions were moving in by 8-9 October.
rationale: A static opening garrison would understate exposure over the whole four-day episode.

assumption_text: The opening Sinai military-death range is bounded using a direct floor, early tank-loss concentration by 9 October, and a cautious share of broader Israeli war losses after subtracting the Golan-front total.
rationale: No direct phase-specific Israeli KIA count was found for 6-9 October, so a transparent model was required after exhausting better front-window sources.

assumption_text: No civilian person-month denominator is estimated for the opening Sinai episode.
rationale: Available evidence supports only qualitative sparsity in the canal battle corridor; using whole-Sinai or national populations would violate the project rules.

assumption_text: Unknown-status deaths are excluded under a strict rule rather than proportionally assigned.
rationale: The prompt requires preserving unknown status when the civilian/military split is weak.

assumption_text: Indirect deaths are not folded into direct military or civilian deaths anywhere in the bundle.
rationale: The project baseline metric is direct violent deaths only.
